figures.caption: Figure 1. Core challenges in long-range cooperation: (a) observation imprecision for distant targets, (b) quadratic scaling costs of dense features, and (c) positional mismatch from alignment errors.
Table 1. Performance comparison on Griffin-25m. Bold, underline, and italic represent the top three among feature-level fusion methods.
Table 2. Performance comparison on V2X-Seq. Bold, underline, and italic represent the top three among feature-level fusion methods.
Figure 2. Performance comparison on Griffin-25m dataset. The X-axis and Y-axis represent perception metrics, while the bubble size and color encode the transmission cost on a logarithmic scale.
Table 3. Detailed ablation study for the GQG module. We report the performance gains of single-agent models on Griffin-25m dataset.

Figure 4. Overview of Long-SCOPE framework, highlighting our novel components: the Geometry-guided Query Generation module for dynamically proposing object queries and the Context-Aware Association module for robust multi-agent query matching.

Figure 6. Comparison of robustness to localization errors on Griffin-25m dataset. Long-SCOPE maintains stable tracking and detection under increasing translation and rotation noise compared to the baseline.
Figure 5. Depth derivation for high-vantage agents. We predict the stable global height ẑQglb (left histogram) instead of the dispersed depth ẑQcam (right histogram) and utilize geometric priors.
